1.title: The Magic Blue Bin
1.fact: Singapore has special blue recycling bins almost everywhere (kind of like bubble tea shops!) which are never too far away. These bins love eating paper, plastic bottles, metal cans, and glass jars.

2.title: Wash Before You Toss
2.fact: Before you put a bottle or can into the blue bin, give it a little rinse! Food leftovers on your recyclables can make the whole bin 'dirty', which means nothing in it can be recycled.

3.title: Bags Don't Belong
3.fact: Even though plastic bags are made of plastic, they are not allowed to be thrown in the blue bin. This is because thin plastics or food items like pizza boxes have greasy food stuck on them, making them unsuitable for recycling.

4.title: The 40% Waste Warning
4.fact: About 40% of items put into blue recycling bins are wasted because they are either dirty (like food-stained containers) or things that can't be recycled at all.

5.title: Singapore's Halfway Mark
5.fact: Singapore's recycling rate is about 50%, which means half of all the waste produced by homes and businesses gets recycled!

6.title: Old Gadget Hideouts
6.fact: Old phones, tablets, and other electronic gadgets shouldn't go in the blue bin. They have their own very special e-waste bins scattered all around the island!

7.title: Singapore's Trash Island
7.fact: Singapore's only landfill is on a special island called Pulau Semakau, and it's expected to be completely full by 2035! The less rubbish we make, the longer Semakau will last to stay a beautiful home for nature.

8.title: The Power of Food Waste
8.fact: Food waste in Singapore doesn't have to just rot away. It can be turned into rich compost soil or even biofuel through special processes like anaerobic digestion or innovative insect farming!

9.title: The Power of the 3Rs
9.fact: The 3Rs mean Reduce, Reuse, and Recycle! They are listed in order of how powerful they are. Reducing waste (using less) is the most powerful, reusing comes second, and recycling is the last resort.
